title: Weekly Marketing Planning & Goals
summary: Ziad Al Khatib presented visual advertising content for Wednesday and Thursday, including a graphic referencing "9 hours to 5 minutes real results," which Michael Donovan questioned for factual accuracy and emphasized the need for system fact-checking of quantitative metrics. Michael Donovan and Ziad Al Khatib discussed the evolving content strategy, outlining Perplexity settings, content requirements like "fact check quantitative metrics and statistics" and "Punchy lines," and agreed on an optional 250-character limit for content length, particularly for Facebook. The speakers also addressed technical issues related to file size limits and project file management, reviewed content performance and document strategy where Michael Donovan requested previous creative posts in a PDF format from Ziad Al Khatib, and examined an AI-generated output, with Michael Donovan questioning the AI's data connections and noting the importance of understanding the AI's structure to consistently achieve content objectives.

decisions.description: Optional 250-character limit for content length, particularly for Facebook.
Remove example output to reduce project file size.
Previous creative posts to be sent in PDF format.

actionItems.task: Select, import, and send previous creative posts to Michael Donovan in PDF format.

tensions: Michael Donovan questioned the factual accuracy of the '9 hours to 5 minutes' statistic.
Michael Donovan questioned the AI's data connections and relevance of its output.
Technical issues with saving files due to being 'over' the size limit.
referencesToPast: Michael Donovan noted the system had previously allowed saving a file with the same high number count.
Ziad Al Khatib suggested attachments might be pulling from previous topics.

contributions.description: Presented visual advertising content for Wednesday and Thursday.
Emphasized the need for system fact-checking of quantitative metrics.
Outlined Perplexity settings and content requirements like 'Punchy lines'.
Suggested an optional 250-character limit for Facebook content length.
Offered to send previous creative posts to Michael Donovan.
Clarified AI output structure: headline is first line, image copy for visual.
Decided to remove example output to reduce file size.
